workflow.name: Generate two-host PDF podcasts with GPT-5, Smallest AI, and Gmail
workflow.description:
### Generate two-host AI podcast episodes from any PDF — in 9 Indian languages or your own cloned voice — powered by Smallest AI.

Turn whitepapers, research reports, blog drafts, lecture notes, or company memos into NotebookLM-style audio briefings. Native Hindi, Tamil, Telugu, Kannada, Malayalam, Bengali, Marathi, Gujarati, and Punjabi support makes this the only n8n template that ships authentic Indian-language podcast generation out of the box.

**Good to know**

- Lightning V3.1 generates ~100ms time-to-first-byte; a 5-page PDF produces a 4-minute podcast in roughly 60 seconds total
- Smallest AI offers a free tier — get an [API key](https://app.smallest.ai/dashboard/api-keys)
- Voice cloning lets you cast yourself or a colleague as one of the hosts using just a 10-second voice sample
- Pure JavaScript audio concatenation — no FFmpeg required, works on n8n Cloud

## How it works

- Upload a PDF via the form trigger
- Extract text and pass it to GPT-5 to generate a structured 2-host script in JSON
- Split the script and route each turn to the matching host's TTS branch
- Smallest AI Lightning synthesizes each turn at 24 kHz with sub-100ms latency
- A Code node concatenates all WAV chunks into a single audio file in correct conversational order
- The finished podcast is emailed to the address you specify

## Set up steps

- Install the `n8n-nodes-smallestai` community node via **Settings → Community Nodes**
- Connect Smallest AI, OpenAI, and Gmail credentials (~5 minutes)
- Pick voice IDs for Host A and Host B in the two Synthesize Host nodes (defaults: `avery` and `devansh`)
- Update the recipient email in the Gmail node
- Sticky notes inside the workflow walk you through each section

## Requirements

- **Smallest AI** account — free tier available at [smallest.ai](https://smallest.ai)
- **OpenAI** API key — GPT-5 or GPT-4o-mini both work
- **Gmail** OAuth2 credential for delivery
- **n8n-nodes-smallestai** community node installed

## Customising this workflow

- Swap the Form trigger for a Webhook to integrate with other apps or chatbots
- Change the LLM system prompt to alter podcast format (interview, debate, monologue, kids' story)
- Edit `english` in the prompt to generate podcasts in any of Smallest AI's supported languages — try `arjun` + `aanya` for Hindi, `arvind` + `niharika` for Tamil
- Use cloned voices from the Voice Clone resource for personalized hosts
- Replace Gmail with Slack, Telegram, or Drive Upload for different delivery channels

workflow.name: PDF to AI Podcast in 9 Indian Languages with Smallest AI

parameters.content:
## 🎙️ PDF to Podcast — Powered by Smallest AI

Convert any PDF into a NotebookLM-style two-host podcast, delivered to your inbox. Generate audio in 19+ languages including 9 Indian tongues — Hindi, Tamil, Telugu, Kannada, Malayalam, Bengali, Marathi, Gujarati, Punjabi.

### Required credentials (~5 min setup)

- **Smallest AI** — get yours [API Key](https://app.smallest.ai/dashboard/api-keys) (free tier available)
- **OpenAI** API key — GPT-5 or GPT-4o-mini
- **Gmail** OAuth2 for delivery

### Required community node

This template uses `n8n-nodes-smallestai`. Install via **Settings → Community Nodes** before importing.

### How it works

1. Form trigger captures the PDF
2. GPT-5 generates a structured 2-host JSON script
3. Switch routes each turn to the correct voice
4. Smallest AI Lightning synthesizes each turn (~100ms TTFB)
5. Code node concatenates WAV chunks in order
6. Gmail emails the finished podcast

Pure JavaScript audio concat — no FFmpeg, works on n8n Cloud.

### Customise

- **Voices:** Edit `voiceId` in the two Synthesize Host nodes. Defaults: `avery` (Host A) + `devansh` (Host B). Browse all the [voices](https://docs.smallest.ai/waves/v-4-0-0/api-reference/api-reference/voices/get-waves-voices).
- **Language:** Change `english` in the system prompt. Both voices must match.
  - Hindi: `arjun` + `aanya` · Tamil: `arvind` + `niharika` · Bengali: `arnab` + `ishita`
- **Length & format:** Edit the "4-minute / 25-35 turns" rule or rewrite cast personas (interview, debate, kids' story).
- **Recipient:** Update the email in "Email Podcast to User".
- **Delivery:** Swap Gmail for Slack, Telegram, or Drive Upload.

### WAV format consistency

Don't override `sample_rate` per call in the Smallest AI nodes — keep both Synthesize Host nodes on identical config or the concat will produce noise.

## 1. Upload PDF

Submits a PDF via the form. Extract from File pulls plain text and hands it to the script generator.

## 2. Generate the Podcast Script

GPT-5 turns the PDF text into a structured 2-host JSON script. Schema is enforced via the OpenAI node's built-in JSON Schema mode.

## 3. Synthesize With the Right Voice

Switch routes each turn by `host` (A or B). Each branch calls Smallest AI Lightning with a different voice ID. Edit Fields preserve `order` and `host` for re-sorting downstream.

## 4. Stitch Audio in Order

Merge collects both branches, Sort restores conversational order via the `order` field, and the Code node concatenates all WAV chunks into a single audio file.

**Pure JavaScript** — no FFmpeg required, works on n8n Cloud.

## 5. Deliver via Email

Gmail attaches the WAV and emails it to the recipient.

**Update the `To` field** — currently set to a placeholder.

Swap Gmail for Slack, Telegram, or Drive Upload as needed.

parameters.formDescription: Upload a PDF and receive a two-host AI podcast by email.

values.content:
=You are scripting a 4-minute podcast in english. Aim for 25-35 short conversational turns total.

CAST:
- Host A: Avery, a young female American host. Curious, asks probing
  questions, plays the "interested generalist."
- Host B: Devansh, a young male Indian host. The "expert" who explains
  concepts, reacts to A's questions, occasionally challenges A.

DIALOGUE RULES:
1. Hosts MUST react to each other. B never just delivers a paragraph —
   B responds to what A just said, then adds new information.
2. Average turn length: 1-3 sentences. Long monologues are forbidden.
3. Use connective tissue: "Right, so...", "Wait, but...", "Hmm, push back
   on that...", "That's a great point — and actually..."
4. Include 3+ moments of interruption, qualification, or clarifying questions.
5. Open: A welcomes listeners, introduces B + topic. Close: B summarizes,
   A signs off.
6. Snappy back-and-forth is the goal.

Number each turn sequentially in the `order` field. Output JSON matching
the schema.

parameters.message: Your generated podcast is attached. Powered by Smallest AI.
parameters.subject: Your AI Podcast is Ready 🎙️

user.name: Sumit Mor
user.bio: I’m a Technical Write & devrel at Smallest AI, I work at the intersection of developer experience, technical content, and community.
